headings: IV. Insurance Policy Deductibles and Self-Insured Retentions.
text: - c. CGL Deductible Contribution. In the event of an 'occurrence' of 'bodily injury' or  'property  damage'  or  a  'personal  injury'  or  'advertising  injury  offense,'  as those terms are defined in the OCIP CGL Policy, which requires Owner to satisfy all or any portion of the deductibles or self-insured retentions set forth in the OCIP CGL Policy (the  'CGL  Deductible')  and  which  arises  out  of  or  is  in  any  way connected with the Work, whether during construction or after completion of the Work or both, the Owner may, in its sole discretion, seek contribution(s) towards such CGL Deductible (the 'CGL Deductible Contribution') from any ' Involved Party,'  in  a  total  amount  not  to  exceed  $ 50,000.00  per  occurrence.  The  CGL Deductible  Contribution  shall  include,  without  limitation,  attorneys'  fees,  court costs, and other defense expenses.
